title: Latest News from Science Magazine
description: Keyword search result for Alerts: News Weekly Headlines -- New results matching your keyword search query (updated daily).

items.title: Amid a flood of AI advances, astrophysicists are questioning the soul of their field
items.description: Researchers see enormous power in new tools—but also the potential end of astrophysics as a human endeavor

items.title: Bees just did something no other insect has been shown to do
items.description: Like humans, bumble bees can plan ahead when working through a problem, even pausing to consider the solution

items.title: U.S. health agencies join fight against ‘ultraprocessed foods’—whatever they are
items.description: ome scientists criticize label as ambiguous and confusing; others call it a valuable lens on diet

items.title: Democrats on House China committee decry dismissal of National Science Board
items.description: Letter also blames Trump for letting China surpass United States in research spending

items.title: NIH scientists plead not guilty to smuggling monkeypox viruses into U.S.
items.description: Defendants face prison time for criminal charges but samples may all have been inactivated and used for routine diagnostics

items.title: After empty promises, string theory finds new uses
items.description: Physicists hope back-to-basics approach could point toward alternative paths to “theory of everything”

items.title: Mathematicians issue warning as AI rapidly gains ground
items.description: New declaration argues the technology jeopardizes the field’s values and culture

items.title: Doubling down on controversial claims, Microsoft accelerates quantum computing plans
items.description: Company says its improved topological qubits will lead to a practical machine in 3 years—as critics scoff

items.title: How Europe hopes to lure foreign researchers with massive new grants
items.description: Maria Leptin, president of the European Research Council, on supergrants, AI-generated submissions, and the agency’s future

items.title: Radical proposal would block solar storms with orbital ‘airbag’
items.description: Gases released from satellites could slash the threat of severe space weather
